name: outside-writer-fork-boundary
description: Documented boundary: a writer that appends to the corpus without routing through the daemon sits outside the serialization boundary and can still fork the chain (here: an outside record signed against a stale genesis tail after the daemon already extended the chain). Corpus-scoped tail resolution narrows this window; only daemon routing eliminates it. The daemon cannot repair outside forks, but its own subsequent writes remain single-parented on the newest tail.

expected.boundary: outside-daemon writers stay outside the serialization boundary; daemon routing is the fix
